articles.author: Sarah Perez
articles.title: Reddit takes on the bots with new ‘human verification’ requirements for fishy behavior - TechCrunch
articles.description: Reddit will require suspected automated accounts to verify they’re human, as it ramps up efforts to curb bot-driven spam and manipulation.
articles.content:
Would-be Reddit competitor Digg just shut down because it couldn’t get a handle on the bots overrunning its site. On Wednesday, Reddit said it’s taking on the challenge itself. 
The company will beg… [+3757 chars]
